page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The CPU configuration allocates exactly one thread per core, ensuring dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading.

This instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, requiring network-attached storage for operating systems and persistent data. There are no graphics processing units (GPUs) bundled with this instance type.

Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps. The absence of local storage and hardware accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance away from high-speed local caching or deep learning workloads. However, the dedicated core allocation and high memory-to-vCPU ratio offer predictable compute performance and substantial memory capacity.

The ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as medium-to-large databases, in-memory caching layers, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and memory-bound data processing pipelines. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core model combined with a high memory density provides a balanced resource profile for enterprise applications that require consistent CPU performance and large memory footprints without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr7.12xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs using Intel Xeon 8369HB processors running at 3.3 GHz, with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing applications. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable performance, making it a stable choice for workloads requiring consistent compute and high memory density without hyperthreading overhead.
